equivalence_argument: No complete mapping: scheduled incident trace types carry an error_chain vector, but payload code has no bounded collect_error_chain_messages helper, depth cap, or truncation marker.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Payload-builder error chain capture is not bounded to 32 entries and does not append the required truncation marker because the collector is absent.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-metrics/src/scheduling.rs stores an error_chain vector in scheduled incident traces.
crates/rpc/rpc-api/src/ritual.rs exposes errorChain in RPC debug types.
Search found no collect_error_chain_messages helper, MAX_ERROR_CHAIN_DEPTH constant, source() traversal in payload builder, or truncation marker.

notes: Existing error_chain fields are storage/exposure only; they do not prove bounded source traversal.
rationale: The primary diff introduces MAX_ERROR_CHAIN_DEPTH = 32, decrements a remaining counter during source traversal, and adds '<error chain truncated after 32 entries>' when the cap is hit. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: collect_error_chain_messages must walk at most 32 error/source entries and append a truncation marker when the cap is reached, preventing malformed or cyclic error chains from consuming unbounded payload-builder time.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build an error chain longer than 32 sources, call collect_error_chain_messages, and assert the result contains 32 error messages plus the truncation marker and terminates even if the chain would otherwise continue.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create an execution error with more than 32 sources and call the expected collector; no bounded collector/truncation marker exists locally.
title: Error-chain capture is bounded
